steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage opens with the warning that introducing a new species can often be harmful, then pivots: waxbills spread seeds of endangered native plants. The blank must complete a logical conclusion — one that follows directly from that seed-spreading evidence and addresses the opening concern.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'non-native birds are unlikely to eat fruits from non-native plants': The passage says nothing about what the waxbills eat; it concerns what they spread. Unsupported. ✗
  • Choice B — 'non-native birds have had both positive and negative effects': No negative effect of waxbills is mentioned; introducing a negative claim without evidence misreads the passage. ✗
  • Choice C — 'although non-native birds initially damaged plants, they are now proving useful': The passage never describes an initial damage phase for the waxbills. ✗
  • Choice D — 'rather than harming Oahu's ecosystem, some non-native birds provide an important benefit': Directly inverts the opening concern using the seed-spreading evidence as proof of benefit. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
